conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> As Player RED, I own the object X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will start with a price slightly above my cost to entice a buyer, perhaps 50 ZUP, leaving room for negotiation. I need to ensure I don't sell below 40 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have the object X which I produced. Since it cost me 40 ZUP to make, I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This is a fair starting price. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 40 ZUP. This is exactly my break-even point (production cost). My goal is to sell for a profit, so accepting 40 ZUP means I make 0 profit. However, rejecting might lead to the game ending with no sale if they don't budge, or I might run out of proposal turns. I have 1 proposal left. I should propose a middle ground, say 45 ZUP. If they reject that, I will have to decide between accepting 40 (no profit) or rejecting (no sale). Since 45 is a small step up from 40 and still a reasonable profit, I will propose that. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your budget constraints, but 40 ZUP is exactly what I spent to produce this. I cannot sell at a loss or break even if I want to continue trading. How about we meet in the middle at 45 ZUP? This gives me a small profit and you get the object for less than my original ask. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED, 45 ZUP is a fair offer. I can go up to 42 ZUP, let me know if that works for you. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 42 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 42 ZUP gives me a 2 ZUP profit. I have used 2 proposals already, and this is my 3rd and final proposal. If I propose a new price (e.g., 43 or 44) and they reject it, I will have no proposals left and will be forced to either accept their last offer (42) or reject the deal entirely (resulting in 0 profit). Since 42 ZUP is a guaranteed profit and they have shown willingness to move up from 40, it is strategically best to accept this offer now rather than risk a stalemate. I cannot make a new proposal after this turn. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds fair to me. I accept your offer of 42 ZUP. Let's finalize the trade. </message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.


You are Player BLUE.

<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player RED, I appreciate the offer. However, I can only offer 40 ZUP for the object X at this time. </message>
